title: narcissus × odorus subsp. assoanus
intro: Narcissus × odorus subsp. assoanus, also known as Asso's daffodil, is a subspecies of the Narcissus × odorus plant, which is a hybrid of the wild daffodil. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. The health benefits of Narcissus × odorus subsp. assoanus include its potential to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of essential oils, teas, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
